serving.natural_language_portion: One individual Valrhona hazelnut tart piece, approximately 80 g, as sold by Kazo Premium at SGD 5.50.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate hazelnut tart — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Tart shell (pastry, rich shortcrust), dark chocolate ganache, hazelnut praline filling
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Valrhona hazelnut tart exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values estimated by weighted composition of: (1) rich shortcrust pastry shell (~35% by weight, USDA SR Legacy 18334), (2) dark chocolate ganache filling (~40% by weight, USDA SR Legacy 19904 dark chocolate + heavy cream), (3) hazelnut praline paste (~25% by weight, USDA SR Legacy 12120 hazelnuts + sugar). Per-100g values derived from weighted average of components. Confidence: medium.

gi_source.title: Chocolate cake and pastry GI values — analogous estimate based on chocolate hazelnut-filled pastry tart; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Valrhona hazelnut tart. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous chocolate-filled pastry tarts from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Rich shortcrust pastry with high fat and hazelnut content slows glucose absorption, supporting a low GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 35.2) / 100 = 15.8 (medium GL). Serving carbohydrate: 44.0 g/100g × 80g serving = 35.2g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~76 mg per serving) and high saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this tart to occasional treats, as the saturated fat content (approximately 11.8 g per serving) may contribute to arterial stiffness, and sodium intake should be monitored within the daily 2000 mg limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~21.2 g per serving) and moderate glycemic load (GL ~15.8)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this tart sparingly; the high sugar and refined carbohydrate content (~35 g carbohydrate per serving) with a moderate glycemic load of approximately 15.8 can cause meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion control is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~14.8 g/100g) and cholesterol (~68 mg/100g) from butter, cream, and chocolate
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this tart as an occasional indulgence only; the high saturated fat content (approximately 11.8 g per serving) exceeds half the recommended daily saturated fat limit of 20 g, and regular consumption may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels and cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Valrhona Hazelnut Tart is a premium French-inspired patisserie dessert sold in Singapore, notably by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall. It consists of a rich, buttery shortcrust pastry shell filled with a smooth Valrhona dark chocolate ganache and hazelnut praline cream. Valrhona is a renowned French chocolate manufacturer known for high-quality couverture chocolate with complex cocoa flavour profiles. The tart is characterised by a crisp, crumbly pastry base, an intensely rich and bittersweet chocolate ganache centre, and a nutty hazelnut praline layer that provides textural contrast and a roasted, caramelised sweetness. The dessert is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a single-portion individual tart. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 468 kcal per 100g, with high fat content (30.5 g/100g, of which 14.8 g is saturated fat) from butter, cream, and chocolate, and moderate carbohydrate content (44 g/100g) with significant sugar (26.5 g/100g). The hazelnut component contributes beneficial monounsaturated fats, vitamin E, magnesium, and iron. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~15.8) due to the portion size. Clinically, this tart is high in saturated fat and sugar, making it a concern for individuals managing cardiovascular disease, Type 2 Diabetes, or hypertension. It contains gluten, dairy, eggs, and tree nuts (hazelnut), and is not suitable for individuals with these allergies. It is vegetarian but not vegan or halal-certified. Priced at SGD 5.50 per piece on GrabFood Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Valrhona hazelnut tart exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or any primary regional database. Values are estimated from weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for shortcrust pastry, dark chocolate, cream, and hazelnuts. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar chocolate-filled pastry tarts. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-based estimation methodology is systematic and the source data for each component is from an approved primary source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component composition (shortcrust pastry shell ~35%, dark chocolate ganache ~40%, hazelnut praline ~25%) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries. No exact branded product entry exists in any approved primary source. 2. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate for a chocolate hazelnut-filled pastry tart based on the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables; no direct measurement for this specific product exists. 3. Halal status is listed as false because Kazo Premium does not appear on the MUIS Singapore halal-certified establishment list as of the processing date; this should be verified with the restaurant directly. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not directly measured for this product. 5. Serving size of 80g is an estimate for a single individual patisserie tart; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 6. The food_id GD001995 is preserved from the input seed record.
